signals.headline: Germany Inflation** | March 2026 data confirmed
signals.raw:
Germany Inflation** | March 2026 data confirmed
Germany's **annual inflation rate climbed to 2.7% in March 2026**[1], up from 1.9% in February, marking the highest level since January 2024. This was driven by a 7.2% spike in energy prices, with fuel costs surging 20% and light heating oil jumping 44.4%[1]. Trading Economics projects inflation to reach 3.1% by end of Q2 2026[1].
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: **NJ-11 Special Election (April 16):** Democrat Analilia Mejia and Republican Joe Hathaway are competing in a special el
signals.raw: **NJ-11 Special Election (April 16):** Democrat Analilia Mejia and Republican Joe Hathaway are competing in a special election scheduled for Thursday, April 16, 2026.[1][2] Recent controversy involved Mejia declining multiple in-person debate opportunities before agreeing to a virtual debate on Wednesday evening.[2]

signals.headline: South Korea GDP Growth** | January 2026 forecast
signals.raw:
South Korea GDP Growth** | January 2026 forecast
The **IMF raised South Korea's 2026 growth forecast to 1.9%**[2], up from a previous 1.8% estimate, driven by semiconductor sector recovery and AI investment expansion. South Korea's economy is projected to grow around 1.9% in 2026, up from 1.0% in 2025[4].
